message:
test(fcp-core): pin ProvisioningRecipe step-kind ordering invariants (flywheel_connectors-6tcjg)

Lands the pinning test for bead flywheel_connectors-6tcjg
("ConnectorPlan ordering"). The bead's "ConnectorPlan" name doesn't
correspond to any literal type in fcp-core; the closest analogue is
ProvisioningRecipe (provisioning.rs:91), which carries a
Vec<ProvisioningStep> with a per-step ProvisioningStepType — the six
"step kinds" PromptUser / PromptSecret / OpenUrl / StoreSecret /
Oauth / Webhook (provisioning.rs:187).

Pinned invariants (test header records the bead-text vs.
type-reality reconciliation in detail):

1. Insertion order is preserved through JSON + CBOR round-trip.
   Steps come back in the same sequence in which they were added
   via .with_step. Combined with provisioning_recipe_display_serde's
   shape pinning, this locks the steps array's positional contract
   end-to-end.

2. The dependency partial order via `depends_on` participates in the
   serialized form (a step that depends_on a previous step's
   StepId carries the link verbatim through the round-trip).

3. The canonical serde tag form per ProvisioningStepType variant is
   pinned positionally — the JSON shape places `kind: "<tag>"` in
   the order the variants appear in the source, so downstream
   tooling that reads steps positionally relies on the existing
   internal-tag form not silently reordering.

The test is purely structural — no production code changes, no logic
edits to ProvisioningRecipe or ProvisioningStepType. ProvisioningStepType
intentionally still does NOT derive Ord (the bead text's "ordering"
phrasing notwithstanding); locking insertion / dependency / serde-tag
ordering is the actual durable contract, and this test gates it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
